<!DOCTYPE qgis PUBLIC 'http://mrcc.com/qgis.dtd' 'SYSTEM'>
<qgis version="3.34.4-Prizren">
  <identifier>psde2.MDC.Zone_poly_U</identifier>
  <parentidentifier></parentidentifier>
  <language>ENG</language>
  <type>dataset</type>
  <title>psde2.MDC.Zone_poly_U</title>
  <abstract>County zone boundaries and overlays derived from the Zoning Maps and other layers from the Parcel Model.

Shows Zoning boundaries in the county 

Procedures_Used: Created by combining and tracing polygons from the Parcels and Lot layers as described in Recorded Resolution. Revisions: Date: Continually revised By: Maintenance application Reason: Change Reviews_Applied_to_Data: An on-line maintenance application used by the Planning and Zoning GIS Section keeps the data updated by applying new Resolutions that change the Zoning Boundaries. Related_Spatial_and_Tabular_Data_Sets: 1- Parcel polygons 2- Lot lines 3 - Section lines (TTRRSS) 4. zone.lyr 3- TBL_ZONES_DADE Other_References_Cited: Engineering Section Sheets &amp; PZ Zoning maps. Notes: NONE</abstract>
  <keywords vocabulary="gmd:topicCategory">
    <keyword>Zoning</keyword>
  </keywords>
  <contact>
    <contactAddress>
      <type>mailing address</type>
      <address>5680 SW 87 Avenue</address>
      <city>Miami</city>
      <administrativearea>FL</administrativearea>
      <postalcode>33173</postalcode>
      <country>USA</country>
    </contactAddress>
    <name></name>
    <organization></organization>
    <position></position>
    <voice>(305) 275-7748</voice>
    <fax></fax>
    <email>bitorre@miamidade.gov</email>
    <role>Point of contact</role>
  </contact>
  <links>
    <link type="" name="Feature Class" format="" size="" url="withheld" mimeType="" description=""/>
  </links>
  <dates/>
  <fees></fees>
  <constraints type="Access">Derivative. </constraints>
  <rights>This dataset should not be used for applications requiring accurate plat survey information such as zone and overlay dimension and area. </rights>
  <encoding></encoding>
  <crs>
    <spatialrefsys nativeFormat="Wkt">
      <wkt>PROJCRS["NAD83 / Florida East (ftUS)",BASEGEOGCRS["NAD83",DATUM["North American Datum 1983",ELLIPSOID["GRS 1980",6378137,298.257222101,LENGTHUNIT["metre",1]]],PRIMEM["Greenwich",0,ANGLEUNIT["degree",0.0174532925199433]],ID["EPSG",4269]],CONVERSION["SPCS83 Florida East zone (US survey foot)",METHOD["Transverse Mercator",ID["EPSG",9807]],PARAMETER["Latitude of natural origin",24.3333333333333,ANGLEUNIT["degree",0.0174532925199433],ID["EPSG",8801]],PARAMETER["Longitude of natural origin",-81,ANGLEUNIT["degree",0.0174532925199433],ID["EPSG",8802]],PARAMETER["Scale factor at natural origin",0.999941177,SCALEUNIT["unity",1],ID["EPSG",8805]],PARAMETER["False easting",656166.667,LENGTHUNIT["US survey foot",0.304800609601219],ID["EPSG",8806]],PARAMETER["False northing",0,LENGTHUNIT["US survey foot",0.304800609601219],ID["EPSG",8807]]],CS[Cartesian,2],AXIS["easting (X)",east,ORDER[1],LENGTHUNIT["US survey foot",0.304800609601219]],AXIS["northing (Y)",north,ORDER[2],LENGTHUNIT["US survey foot",0.304800609601219]],USAGE[SCOPE["Engineering survey, topographic mapping."],AREA["United States (USA) - Florida - counties of Brevard; Broward; Clay; Collier; Dade; Duval; Flagler; Glades; Hendry; Highlands; Indian River; Lake; Martin; Monroe; Nassau; Okeechobee; Orange; Osceola; Palm Beach; Putnam; Seminole; St Johns; St Lucie; Volusia."],BBOX[24.41,-82.33,30.83,-79.97]],ID["EPSG",2236]]</wkt>
      <proj4>+proj=tmerc +lat_0=24.3333333333333 +lon_0=-81 +k=0.999941177 +x_0=200000.0001016 +y_0=0 +ellps=GRS80 +towgs84=0,0,0,0,0,0,0 +units=us-ft +no_defs</proj4>
      <srsid>218</srsid>
      <srid>2236</srid>
      <authid>EPSG:2236</authid>
      <description>NAD83 / Florida East (ftUS)</description>
      <projectionacronym>tmerc</projectionacronym>
      <ellipsoidacronym>EPSG:7019</ellipsoidacronym>
      <geographicflag>false</geographicflag>
    </spatialrefsys>
  </crs>
  <extent>
    <spatial crs="EPSG:2236" maxz="0" maxx="948732.58200199995189905" dimensions="2" minx="763348.57000199996400625" miny="328225.65134799998486415" maxy="597642.31247200001962483" minz="0"/>
  </extent>
</qgis>
